steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject is a singular noun phrase: Valerie Hollister with an appositive phrase. The blank requires a form of the verb “be” to pair with the past participle included in this passive voice construction.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: “Is” is correct because subject-verb agreement requires a singular subject to take a singular verb form. The other three choices are all plural forms (are, were, have been) that violate this rule. That matches choice A — A is correct.
